page: Amazon Web Services r5n.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type.

This instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates that the CPU performance is generally positioned in the lower tiers, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics falling into the bottom 25%. Similarly, Geekbench multi-core scores and Passmark CPU scores are categorized as weak. However, the Geekbench single-core score and Passmark memory score achieve average performance ratings, reflecting a more stable baseline for single-threaded tasks and memory-subsystem operations.

The r5n.xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, which is beneficial for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dense compute resources. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is characterized by its high resource density in terms of memory capacity relative to its 4 vCPUs, though users face a tradeoff with lower overall CPU and memory bandwidth performance as shown in benchmarks. This profile is suitable for workloads such as memory-cached databases, moderate Redis deployments, and general-purpose memory-intensive applications that can operate within a 4 Gbps network baseline.
description: The Amazon Web Services r5n.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores and is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. No local storage or GPU accelerators are included. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show weak ratings for multi-core CPU tasks and memory bandwidth, though single-core Geekbench and Passmark memory scores reach average levels. This resource profile presents a tradeoff of high memory density against lower compute and memory bandwidth speeds. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as caching layers, database operations, and applications requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring a Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual instance featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.1 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Nitro hypervisor architecture with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Average single-core and memory benchmark performance with weaker multi-core results
Suited for memory-cached databases, Redis deployments, and memory-intensive applications
